mainmatter.p: It is clear that a homomorphism of algebras must preserve the bottom element. On the other hand, suppose that { A } \xrightarrow {{ f }}{ B } preserves the bottom element to check that the following diagram commutes:

embedded-tex.embedded-tex-body: \DiagramSquare { nw = TA, ne = TB, sw = A, se = B, north = Tf, south = f, west = \alpha , east = \beta , }

mainmatter.p: Because both composites preserve the bottom element, it is enough to observe that they both take u \in TA to the supremum of the following semidirected subset:

Let { \mathopen {} \left ( A, \alpha \right ) \mathclose {}} and { \mathopen {} \left ( B, \beta \right ) \mathclose {}} be two \mathbb {L}-algebras; then a morphism of dcpos { A } \xrightarrow {{ f }}{ B } is a homomorphism of algebras if and only if it preserves the bottom element.
